Harold and the Purple Crayon (2024)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $17,640,924Details
International Box Office $8,893,539
Worldwide Box Office $26,534,463
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $321,131
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $470,014
Total $791,145
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Inside of his book, adventurous Harold can make anything come to life simply by drawing it. After he grows up and draws himself off the book’s pages and into the physical world, Harold finds he has a lot to learn about real life—and that his trusty purple crayon may set off more hilarious hijinks than he thought possible. When the power of unlimited imagination falls into the wrong hands, it will take all of Harold and his friends’ creativity to save both the real world and his own. Harold and the Purple Crayon is the first film adaptation of the beloved children’s classic that has captivated young readers for decades.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$6,003,197 (34.0% of total gross)
Legs:2.94 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:66.5% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$40,000,000 (worldwide box office is 0.7 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,325 opening theaters/3,325 max. theaters, 2.9 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $18,654,765

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: August 2nd, 2024 (Wide) by Sony Pictures
International Releases: July 31st, 2024 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
August 1st, 2024 (Wide), released as Harold and the Purple Crayon (ГАРОЛЬД И ФИОЛЕТОВЫЙ МЕШОК) (Russia (CIS))
August 1st, 2024 (Wide), released as HAROLD A ČAROVNÁ PASTELKA (Slovakia)
August 1st, 2024 (Wide) (Mexico)
August 1st, 2024 (Wide) (Egypt)
Video Release: August 27th, 2024 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG for mild action and thematic elements.
(Rating bulletin 2780 (cert #53499), 4/26/2023)
Running Time: 92 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Development Hell
Filming location (city or town): Atlanta, GA

Subgenre: Family Adventure
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Adventure

Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Inside of his book, adventurous Harold can make anything come to life simply by drawing it. After he grows up and draws himself off the book’s pages and into the physical world, Harold finds he has a lot to learn about real life—and that his trusty purple crayon may set off more hilarious hijinks than he thought possible. When the power of unlimited imagination falls into the wrong hands, it will take all of Harold and his friends’ creativity to save both the real world and his own. Harold and the Purple Crayon is the first film adaptation of the beloved children’s classic that has captivated young readers for decades.
